info.name: Demos Platform Mobile API
info.description: Mobile-friendly Postman collection for Sanctum auth, onboarding, public content, and the current DEMOS API surface. Participation endpoints now require full constituent verification (email + location + identity/legacy verified status) and suspended accounts are blocked from login and engagement actions.

request.description: Create a user and trigger a 6-digit email verification code for the mobile onboarding flow.
Submit the emailed 6-digit code and save the returned bearer token. Suspended accounts are blocked even after a valid code.
Request a fresh verification code after the resend cooldown expires.
Create a Sanctum token for a mobile user with a verified email address. The response includes the next onboarding step, and suspended accounts return HTTP 423.

List federal and state bills. Optional filters: jurisdiction, jurisdiction_type, search, status, and deadline. `jurisdiction` accepts exact codes like `US` or `CA`, plus `federal` and `state`. `search` also accepts the aliases `q` and `query`.

Fetch a bill with constituent totals, percentages, representative votes, top amendment proposals, whether voting is still open, and the AI-generated `bill.ai_summary_plain` plus `bill.ai_bill_impact` fields. Both AI fields are plain text and capped at 500 characters. User-submitted amendments in `bill.amendments` also include `support_threshold`, pulled from the admin-configured amendment threshold setting.
Requires a user with completed location verification. Returns district-scoped participation, vote proportions, and statistical validity data for the selected bill.
Requires a constituent-verified user whose account is not suspended. Voting closes at the configured deadline before the official vote date, and comments are limited to 50 words.
Remove the current user's vote while the bill remains open for constituent voting. Suspended accounts receive HTTP 423.
List user-submitted amendment proposals for a bill. Optional filters: category and popular.

Requires full constituent verification and a non-suspended account. User amendments now require `title`, `amendment_text`, and `category`. `submitted_at` is set automatically, and amendment responses include `username` and `submitted_date` for mobile display. Amendment text must be 50-70 words and amendments close once the bill has been officially voted.

List all public user-submitted amendments with pagination. Optional filters: `jurisdiction`, `jurisdiction_type`, `status`, `category`, `popular`, and `per_page`. Multiple `status` and `category` values can be sent as comma-separated strings. Each item includes related `bill`, nested `bill.jurisdiction`, `username`, `submitted_date`, `support_threshold`, derived `status`, and `user_supported`.
Requires full constituent verification and a non-suspended account.
Remove the current user's amendment support record. Suspended accounts receive HTTP 423.

List public citizen proposals with pagination. Optional filters: `jurisdiction`, `jurisdiction_type`, `jurisdiction_focus`, `status`, `category`, `search`, `popular`, and `per_page`. Multiple `status` and `category` values can be sent as comma-separated strings. Each item includes `username`, `submitted_date`, `support_threshold`, derived `status`, derived `jurisdiction_type`, `expires_at`, `days_left`, and `user_supported`.
Fetch a citizen proposal with proposer details and whether the current user already supports it.

Requires full constituent verification and a non-suspended account. Send `problem_statement` plus `proposed_solution` for the new mobile form, or the legacy `content` field for backward compatibility. Citizen proposals are auto-published, and the response now includes enriched fields like `username`, `submitted_date`, `support_threshold`, `status`, and `days_left`.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Expand digital civic access",
  "problem_statement": "Residents in underserved neighborhoods have limited access to reliable internet, devices, and multilingual civic information.",
  "proposed_solution": "Create a public digital access program that funds community internet hubs, device lending, and multilingual civic outreach through libraries and schools.",
  "category": "education",
  "jurisdiction_focus": "federal"
}

request.description: Requires full constituent verification and a non-suspended account.
Remove the current user's proposal support record. Suspended accounts receive HTTP 423.
Fetch published FAQs, guidelines, and announcements. Without filters the API groups the content by type; with type/audience filters it returns a flat items list.
Fetch a single published managed content page by slug. Use this for pages like `privacy-policy` and `terms-of-service`.
Report an amendment or citizen proposal for moderation. Suspended accounts are blocked from reporting.

Fetch the authenticated mobile profile, including `profile_information`, `verification_status`, top-level `email_preferences`, top-level `push_notification_preferences`, onboarding progress, identity verification status, suspension details, and profile image URLs at `profile_photo_url`, `profile_image_url`, and `profile_information.profile_photo_url`.

Update the authenticated user's profile information. Supports `name`/`full_name`, `email`, `phone_number`/`phone`, `address`/`street_address`, `country`, `state`, `district`, `zip_code`/`postal_code`, and `notification_preferences` for backward-compatible generic settings storage. For profile photos, use the separate multipart request below. Changing the email clears email verification until the new address is verified.

Upload or replace the current profile image using multipart form-data. Use `POST /api/user` for file uploads because raw multipart `PUT` uploads are not reliable in PHP. Send the image file as `profile_image`. To remove the image without uploading a new one, send `remove_profile_image=true` to the standard profile update endpoint.
Fetch the mobile Privacy Settings page payload, including privacy policy links and account/privacy control actions.
Fetch the mobile-ready email preferences payload, including the summary copy, normalized toggle values, and section metadata for each email setting.
Update one or more email-preference toggles. Supported fields are `account_updates`, `security_alerts`, `reminders`, `promotions`, and `newsletter`.
Fetch the mobile-ready push notification settings payload, including summary copy, normalized toggle values, and item metadata for each alert type.
Update one or more push notification toggles. Supported fields are `bill_updates`, `significance_alerts`, `weekly_digest`, `proposal_activity`, and `representative_updates`.
Register or update the current device's FCM token for push notifications.
Remove a previously registered FCM device token for the authenticated user.
Send a Firebase Cloud Messaging test push to the authenticated user's registered device tokens. The selected notification type must be enabled for the user.
Soft-delete the authenticated user account and revoke all active Sanctum tokens. Send either `password` or `current_password` to confirm the delete action.
Complete onboarding with manually entered address fields. The API geocodes the address, resolves districts, and advances the user toward constituent verification.

body.raw:
{
  "country": "United States",
  "state": "District of Columbia",
  "district": "Washington",
  "street_address": "1600 Pennsylvania Avenue NW",
  "zip_code": "20500"
}

request.description: Complete onboarding with device coordinates. The API reverse geocodes the location, resolves districts, and advances the user toward constituent verification.
List the current user's bill votes with related bill records.
List the current user's amendment and citizen proposal support records.
List the current user's submitted amendments and citizen proposals.
List federal and state representatives resolved from the user's stored districts.
